V-M-032.checks: Verify configurable VPN addressing, non-10.0.0.0/8 coexistence with OpenVPN, and 500/1000-device capacity behavior.
V-M-032.commands: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_vpn_network_addressing.py tests/test_deploy_network_subnets_static.py tests/test_vpn_service.py tests/test_manual_client_cli.py tests/test_device_api.py -v && cd .. && bash -n install.sh deploy/deploy-on-server.sh deploy/deploy-all.sh deploy/lib/vpn-network.sh
V-M-032.last-run: 25 passed, 9 warnings. Shell syntax gate passed for install.sh, deploy/deploy-on-server.sh, deploy/deploy-all.sh and deploy/lib/vpn-network.sh.
V-M-032.live-run: Operator-confirmed VPS deployment test passed for release v2.13.0-vpn-network-capacity-stable.

verification-levels.level-module-local: Pure Python tests for subnet parsing, gateway derivation, capacity validation, allocation bounds and migration guard decisions.
verification-levels.level-wave: Static deploy-script tests for env propagation, generated AWG addresses, NAT/policy-routing substitutions and absence of hardcoded 10.10/10.200 runtime assumptions.
verification-levels.level-phase: End-to-end dry-run evidence that fresh deploy settings flow into backend config, RU client interface, DE/RU relay interface, manual clients and device API configs.

verification-matrix.matrix-1: Given VPN_CLIENT_SUBNET=172.29.0.0/22, gateway is 172.29.0.1 and the first generated client address is inside 172.29.0.0/22, not 10.0.0.0/8.
verification-matrix.matrix-2: Given VPN_RELAY_SUBNET=172.29.255.0/30, DE address is 172.29.255.1 and RU address is 172.29.255.2.
verification-matrix.matrix-3: VPN_CAPACITY_PROFILE=1000 rejects /24 and accepts /22 or larger after reserved addresses are removed.
verification-matrix.matrix-4: Without VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE, existing VPNClient.address values are not rewritten and generated configs remain tied to stored addresses.
verification-matrix.matrix-5: With VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=1, migration emits required markers and routes all client config changes through existing rotation/reprovision flows.
verification-matrix.matrix-6: Deploy scripts pass bash syntax checks and static tests prove client/relay/NAT/routing values come from validated env variables.

telemetry-requirements.field-1: client_subnet, relay_subnet, capacity_profile, usable_address_count and rotate_enabled must be logged as structured non-secret fields.
telemetry-requirements.field-2: Do not log private keys, preshared keys, full client configs, SSH credentials, tokens, passwords or raw .env contents.
telemetry-requirements.field-3: Overlap detection must identify conflicting CIDRs but must not dump full host route tables into application logs.

scenarios.scenario-1: Fresh deploy with VPN_CLIENT_SUBNET=172.29.0.0/22 configures the server gateway as 172.29.0.1 and allocates clients from 172.29.0.2 onward.
scenarios.scenario-2: Fresh RU/DE relay deploy with VPN_RELAY_SUBNET=172.29.255.0/30 configures DE as 172.29.255.1 and RU as 172.29.255.2.
scenarios.scenario-3: VPN_CAPACITY_PROFILE=1000 rejects /24 pools and accepts /22 or larger pools with at least 1000 usable client addresses.
scenarios.scenario-4: Upgrade without VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE preserves existing VPNClient.address values and does not silently invalidate generated client configs.
scenarios.scenario-5: Explicit VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=1 performs host rule replacement and forces client config reprovisioning through existing rotation flows.
scenarios.scenario-6: Deploy static checks fail if runtime paths retain hardcoded 10.10.0.0/24 or 10.200.0.0/24 assumptions outside documented compatibility references.

module-checks.check: Client and relay subnets can be set to non-10.0.0.0/8 ranges for new deployments.
1000-device capacity cannot be configured with a pool smaller than the validated threshold.
Existing clients are protected from accidental address-pool migration unless the operator enables explicit rotation.
AWG obfuscation and anti-abuse flows remain compatible with generated configs after subnet changes.

autonomous-execution-safety.safe: Autonomous execution may edit config parsing, pure subnet helpers, tests, env examples and static deploy-script generation logic.
autonomous-execution-safety.requires-operator: Live VPS route mutation, actual AWG interface reload, production VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=1 and mass client config rotation require explicit operator approval.
autonomous-execution-safety.stop-if: Stop execution if overlap with active routes is detected, existing client addresses would be rewritten with VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=0, or the selected capacity profile cannot be satisfied.

rollout-policy.step-1: Use the 172.29.0.0/22 client subnet and 172.29.255.0/30 relay subnet for new VPS test deployments.
rollout-policy.step-2: For existing deployments, run first with VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=0 and verify no client addresses are changed.
rollout-policy.step-3: Enable VPN_NETWORK_ROTATE=1 only during a planned maintenance window because all affected clients must download new configs.
